_note: Slab c.1120-700 BC — the BRIDGE from the HERACLEIDAE (existing MYC_ nodes) down to the ARGEAD KINGS OF MACEDON (existing MAC_ line) and to the two Spartan royal houses. Two threads, both hanging off nodes already in the tree. THREAD 1 — MACEDON. The existing Argead line already runs PERDICCAS1MAC (Perdiccas I, c.700, founder of the Macedonian kingship per Herodotus 7.137/8.139) > ARGAEUS1MAC > PHILIP1MAC > AEROPUS1MAC > ALCETAS1MAC > AMYNTAS1MAC > ALEXANDER1MAC 'the Philhellene' > AMYNTASOFALEX > ARRHIDAEUS > AMYNTAS3 (Amyntas III) > PHILIP2 (built in 06_deep_antiquity.json). PERDICCAS1MAC currently has NO parent — it is the parentless top of the Argead chain. This slab therefore does NOT recreate any of those kings (that would duplicate them / double-parent them). Instead it builds ONLY the missing LEGENDARY stretch above Perdiccas I and grafts it on by making PERDICCAS1MAC the child of Tyrimmas — giving the existing chain its first parent, with no double-parenting. AMYNTAS3 is left untouched (it already has its parent ARRHIDAEUS). The new legendary line follows the received classical pedigree of Caranus (recorded in Diodorus and the Argead king-list tradition; the branch that names Coenus & Tyrimmas): the Argive Temenid kings MYC_TEMENUS (existing) > Ceisus > Maron > Thestrus > Acous > Aristodamidas (all Heraclid kings of Argos, kept in the MYCENAE house as MYC_), then the migration north to found Macedon at Aegae — MAC_CARANUS (c.808, the traditional first Argead king per Theopompus) > Coenus > Tyrimmas > and on to the existing Perdiccas I. The whole Temenus->Perdiccas stretch is LEGENDARY/semi-legendary and each node is flagged; the tradition (Herodotus 8.137) making the Macedonian kings Heraclids descended from Temenus of Argos is a dynastic claim, not documented fact. RESULT: a continuous line MYC_TEMENUS > MYC_CEISUS > MYC_MARON > MYC_THESTRUS > MYC_ACOUS > MYC_ARISTODAMIDAS > MAC_CARANUS > MAC_COENUS > MAC_TYRIMMAS > PERDICCAS1MAC > ... > AMYNTAS3 > PHILIP2 — the Greek legends now reach Philip II. THREAD 2 — SPARTA's dual kingship, the two houses from the twin sons of Aristodemus (existing MYC_EURYSTHENES & MYC_PROCLES, c.1090). AGIAD line from Eurysthenes: Agis I (eponym) > Echestratus > Labotas > Doryssus > Agesilaus I > Archelaus > Teleclus. EURYPONTID line from Procles: Soos (a later insertion, likely fictitious) > Eurypon (eponym) > Prytanis > Polydectes > Eunomus > his sons Charilaus (the king) and Lycurgus (c.800, the lawgiver & regent — no crown; his parentage and which king he guarded, the infant Charilaus per Simonides/Plutarch or the Agiad Labotas per Herodotus 1.65, are disputed — noted, not resolved). Father-son links per Herodotus 8.131 (Eurypontid) & the Agiad list; these early Spartan kings are semi-legendary (little evidence before the mid-6th c. BC) and each is flagged. Dates are nominal placeholders that descend from the tree's existing 1090 BC anchor for the twins (older than the traditional c.930 BC reign-dates) so every child year stays smaller than its parent's. Web-verified against Wikipedia 'Caranus of Macedon', 'Argead dynasty', 'List of kings of Sparta', 'Agiad dynasty', 'Eurypontid', and Diodorus's Temenid pedigree. No fabrication — received mythology and the classical king-lists followed; unattested consorts drawn as '—' placeholders rather than invented; disputed points flagged.

families.note: MACEDON (LEGENDARY) — the Argive Temenid line. MYC_TEMENUS (existing: Heraclid who took Argos in the Dorian Return) to his son Ceisus, king of Argos, first of the intermediary Temenid kings in Caranus's received pedigree.

children.label:
👑 Ceisus
c.1085 BC · son of Temenus; king of Argos in the Temenid line · LEGENDARY

families.note: MACEDON (LEGENDARY) — Temenid kings of Argos. Ceisus to Maron.

children.label:
👑 Maron
c.1030 BC · Temenid king of Argos; ancestor of the Macedonian Argeads in the received pedigree · LEGENDARY

families.note: MACEDON (LEGENDARY) — Temenid kings of Argos. Maron to Thestrus.

children.label:
👑 Thestrus
c.975 BC · Temenid of the Argive royal line; ancestor of Caranus · LEGENDARY

families.note: MACEDON (LEGENDARY) — Temenid kings of Argos. Thestrus to Acous.

children.label:
👑 Acous
c.920 BC · Temenid of the Argive royal line; ancestor of Caranus · LEGENDARY

families.note: MACEDON (LEGENDARY) — Temenid kings of Argos. Acous to Aristodamidas, the last Argive-side ancestor before the migration north.

children.label:
👑 Aristodamidas
c.865 BC · Temenid king of Argos; father of Caranus in the received Argead pedigree · LEGENDARY

families.note: MACEDON (LEGENDARY) — the founding of the Argead dynasty. Aristodamidas to CARANUS (MAC_ prefix routes to the ANTIQUITY house), the Heraclid of Argos who migrated north and founded the Macedonian kingdom at Aegae; the traditional first Argead king (Theopompus, Marsyas), distinct from Herodotus's Perdiccas-first tradition.

children.label:
👑 Caranus
c.808 BC · Heraclid of Argos who went north & founded the Argead dynasty at Aegae; the legendary first king of Macedon · LEGENDARY

families.note: MACEDON (LEGENDARY) — Caranus to Coenus, his son and successor in the Argead line.

children.label:
👑 Coenus
c.775 BC · son of Caranus; king of Macedon in the Argead line · LEGENDARY

families.note: MACEDON (semi-legendary) — Coenus to Tyrimmas, an intermediary king inserted before Perdiccas I to extend the Argead line to eight generations from c.808 BC. Tyrimmas is the father of Perdiccas I.

children.label:
👑 Tyrimmas
c.740 BC · son of Coenus; father of Perdiccas I; an intermediary king inserted to lengthen the Argead pedigree · semi-legendary

families.note: MACEDON — THE GRAFT. Tyrimmas to the EXISTING Perdiccas I (PERDICCAS1MAC, built in 06_deep_antiquity.json, c.700, founder of the Macedonian kingship per Herodotus). PERDICCAS1MAC had no parent; this family gives it its first, connecting the legendary Temenid line down into the historical Argead chain PERDICCAS1MAC > ... > AMYNTAS3 > PHILIP2. No node re-created, no double-parenting — PERDICCAS1MAC is referenced, not redefined, and AMYNTAS3 is left with its existing parent ARRHIDAEUS.
SPARTA — the AGIAD royal house (semi-legendary). MYC_EURYSTHENES (existing: elder twin son of Aristodemus, founder of the Agiad line) to Agis I, from whom the house takes its name.

children.label:
👑 Agis I
c.1050 BC · son of Eurysthenes; eponym of the Agiad royal house of Sparta · semi-legendary

families.note: SPARTA — AGIAD line. Agis I to Echestratus.

children.label:
👑 Echestratus
c.1010 BC · son of Agis I; Agiad king of Sparta · semi-legendary

families.note: SPARTA — AGIAD line. Echestratus to Labotas (Leobotas), whom Herodotus (1.65) makes the boy-king for whom Lycurgus served as regent.

children.label:
👑 Labotas
c.965 BC · son of Echestratus; Agiad king; in Herodotus the child-king guarded by his uncle Lycurgus · semi-legendary

families.note: SPARTA — AGIAD line. Labotas to Doryssus.

children.label:
👑 Doryssus
c.920 BC · son of Labotas; Agiad king of Sparta · semi-legendary

families.note: SPARTA — AGIAD line. Doryssus to Agesilaus I.

children.label:
👑 Agesilaus I
c.875 BC · son of Doryssus; Agiad king of Sparta · semi-legendary

families.note: SPARTA — AGIAD line. Agesilaus I to Archelaus (the Agiad, distinct from the Macedonian Archelaus).

children.label:
👑 Archelaus
c.825 BC · son of Agesilaus I; Agiad king who with the Eurypontid Charilaus took Aegys · semi-legendary

families.note: SPARTA — AGIAD line. Archelaus to Teleclus, who pushed Sparta's power south into the Eurotas valley and was killed by the Messenians — the line reaching toward ~750 BC.

children.label:
👑 Teleclus
c.760 BC · son of Archelaus; Agiad king who took Amyclae, Pharis & Geronthrae; slain by Messenians · semi-legendary

families.note: SPARTA — the EURYPONTID royal house (semi-legendary). MYC_PROCLES (existing: younger twin son of Aristodemus, founder of the Eurypontid line) to Soos, a name inserted into the list to balance the two houses and likely fictitious.

children.label:
👑 Soos
c.1050 BC · reputed son of Procles; Eurypontid king; a later insertion, likely fictitious · LEGENDARY

families.note: SPARTA — EURYPONTID line. Soos to Eurypon, from whom the house takes its name.

children.label:
👑 Eurypon
c.1010 BC · son of Soos; eponym of the Eurypontid royal house of Sparta · semi-legendary

families.note: SPARTA — EURYPONTID line. Eurypon to Prytanis.

children.label:
👑 Prytanis
c.960 BC · son of Eurypon; Eurypontid king of Sparta · semi-legendary

families.note: SPARTA — EURYPONTID line (father-son order per Herodotus 8.131). Prytanis to Polydectes (the Spartan king, distinct from the Seriphian Polydectes of myth).

children.label:
👑 Polydectes
c.905 BC · son of Prytanis; Eurypontid king of Sparta · semi-legendary

families.note: SPARTA — EURYPONTID line. Polydectes to Eunomus, father of Charilaus and, in the common tradition, of Lycurgus the lawgiver.

children.label:
👑 Eunomus
c.850 BC · son of Polydectes; Eurypontid king; father of Charilaus & (in tradition) of Lycurgus · semi-legendary

families.note: SPARTA — EURYPONTID line. Eunomus x a wife > two sons: Charilaus, the reigning Eurypontid king, and Lycurgus, the lawgiver. Lycurgus bears no crown (regent, not king); the sources dispute his exact parentage and which king he guarded — the infant Charilaus (Simonides, Plutarch) or the Agiad Labotas (Herodotus 1.65) — so it is flagged, not resolved.

children.label:
👑 Charilaus
c.790 BC · son of Eunomus; Eurypontid king who raided Argos & Tegea; nephew & ward of Lycurgus · semi-legendary

Lycurgus
c.800 BC · younger Eurypontid son (traditionally of Eunomus); the lawgiver who framed Sparta's constitution & served as royal regent — parentage & the king he guarded disputed · semi-legendary
